utility_evaluation.REASONING:
Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 keys. Now check if each is fully specified with all details and costs:
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan provides two sub-items with plan names and monthly/annual costs. Fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides 'photography equipment' coverage with costs, but not explicitly 'home/contents'. However, the item asks for home/contents insurance with costs; covering only photography equipment may not meet the 'home/contents' requirement. So not fully specified per required item.
- travel_coverage: Travel insurance with costs is provided with plan name and monthly/annual costs. Fully specified.
- additional_services: Any enhancement services with costs provided (Telemedicine Services) with monthly/annual costs. Fully specified.
- total_budget: Complete monthly/annual cost breakdown provided. Fully specified.
Thus, 4 of 5 items are fully specified according to the required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists specific items: SecureLife Basic Plan for physical health, SecureLife Basic Plan for mental health, SecureLife Basic Coverage for property (photography equipment), SecureLife Basic Plan Travel Insurance, and Telemedicine Services. I looked up each of these in the ground-truth ratings. Their corresponding ratings are: SecureLife Basic Plan (physical health) = 4 under primary_health_coverage.physical_health; SecureLife Basic Plan (mental health) = 8 under primary_health_coverage.mental_health; SecureLife Basic Coverage (property/home content) = 6 under property_protection.home_content; SecureLife Basic Plan (travel insurance) = 4 under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance; Telemedicine Services = 10 under health_enhancement_services.
